verses.text: 'Who hath believed our report?' The Servant grows up with 'no form nor comeliness,' 'despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows.' Few receive Him. The lowly, suffering Christ was scorned by the very world He came to save.

'Who hath believed our report?' The Servant grows up with 'no form nor comeliness,' 'despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows.' Few receive Him. The lowly, suffering Christ was scorned by the very world He came to save.

'Who hath believed our report?' The Servant grows up with 'no form nor comeliness,' 'despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows.' Few receive Him. The lowly, suffering Christ was scorned by the very world He came to save.

'Surely he hath borne our griefs... he was wounded for our transgressions... and with his stripes we are healed.' Here is the heart of the gospel. Christ suffered in our place, bearing the punishment that brings us peace and healing.

'Surely he hath borne our griefs... he was wounded for our transgressions... and with his stripes we are healed.' Here is the heart of the gospel. Christ suffered in our place, bearing the punishment that brings us peace and healing.
'All we like sheep have gone astray... and the LORD hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.' Our guilt was transferred to Him. Every wanderer's sin was laid on the one sinless Substitute.

Oppressed and afflicted, 'he opened not his mouth,' led 'as a lamb to the slaughter,' and 'made his grave with the wicked... the rich in his death.' He suffered in silence. The innocent Lamb bore injustice without complaint and was buried as foretold.

Oppressed and afflicted, 'he opened not his mouth,' led 'as a lamb to the slaughter,' and 'made his grave with the wicked... the rich in his death.' He suffered in silence. The innocent Lamb bore injustice without complaint and was buried as foretold.

Oppressed and afflicted, 'he opened not his mouth,' led 'as a lamb to the slaughter,' and 'made his grave with the wicked... the rich in his death.' He suffered in silence. The innocent Lamb bore injustice without complaint and was buried as foretold.

Yet 'it pleased the LORD to bruise him,' making his soul an offering for sin; afterward 'he shall see his seed' and 'justify many.' His death was God's saving plan. Out of His sacrifice springs a redeemed people justified before God.

Yet 'it pleased the LORD to bruise him,' making his soul an offering for sin; afterward 'he shall see his seed' and 'justify many.' His death was God's saving plan. Out of His sacrifice springs a redeemed people justified before God.

Therefore God gives Him 'a portion with the great,' for 'he bare the sin of many, and made intercession for the transgressors.' The Servant is exalted. Having borne our sin, the risen Christ now pleads for us at God's right hand.
